news.text: Trump threatens Iran with power plant strikes over Hormuz oil blockade - Reuters
Saudi Arabia orders Iranian military attache, four embassy staff to leave - Reuters
Family of Iranian butcher missing in strike on commercial complex clings to hope - Reuters
3 themes that gripped Wall Street as stocks dropped for a 4th straight week
Trump threatens to deploy ICE agents to airports if DHS shutdown doesn't end, while Elon Musk offers to cover TSA agents' pay
Britain says Cyprus base will not be part of UK-US self-defence deal - Reuters

brief:
◆ Market Overview

Risk-off tone dominates pre-market with SPY down 1.70% to $648.57 as geopolitical tensions escalate. VIX spike to 35.97 (+6.51%) signals fear is back in the driver's seat after four straight weeks of selling pressure.

◆ What to Watch

  • Middle East Escalation: Trump's threats against Iranian power plants over Hormuz blockade could send oil markets into overdrive
  • DHS Shutdown Drama: Airport chaos looms as Trump threatens ICE deployment while Musk offers TSA bailout - travel stocks in crosshairs
  • Weekend Gap Action: With no economic data today, focus shifts to how markets digest geopolitical headlines and whether we see panic selling or dip buying
  • VIX Above 35: Fear gauge at levels that historically mark short-term bottoms - contrarian opportunity or more pain ahead?
⚠ Bear Case
Hormuz Strait handles 20% of global oil - any disruption sends energy costs through the roof while defensive sectors get hammered. REITs already down 3.17% pre-market suggest rate fears mixing with geopolitical risk.
⚡ Key Driver
Oil price action at Sunday night open will set the tone. Watch crude futures - if we gap above $85, energy rotation accelerates while growth stocks get demolished.

◆ Sector Rotation

Classic risk-off rotation underway with only Financials (+0.18%) showing green as rate expectations shift. Tech leading the decline at -2.27% while Utilities crater -4.06% on rate sensitivity. Energy (-0.08%) surprisingly muted given Middle East headlines - watch for reversal at open.

◆ Levels That Matter

  • SPY $645: Critical support from February lows - break triggers algorithmic selling wave
  • QQQ $575: Tech's line in the sand - violation opens door to $550 measured move
  • VIX $40: Panic threshold that historically marks capitulation bottoms
  • XLE $95: Energy breakout level if oil situation deteriorates further

Not a prediction. A preparation. Make your plan before the bell.
